dare: Write your partner a three-sentence love note and read it aloud.
Give your partner a 60-second shoulder massage.
Tell your partner three things you are grateful for about them.
Hold hands without letting go for the next three rounds.
Draw a portrait of your partner in 60 seconds and show them.
Recite the nicest voicemail or text you have ever received from them.
Name five things you love about your partner without repeating yourself.
Give your partner a genuine compliment about something they rarely get praised for.
Recreate your first hug.
Say something kind to your partner in a different language.
Make a list of three things you are looking forward to doing with them.
Sing happy birthday to your partner but replace the lyrics with compliments.
Tell your partner what you first noticed about them.
Write their name on your hand and keep it visible for the rest of the game.
Do your best impression of how your partner says your name.
Give your partner a hand massage for 30 seconds.
Share the emoji you use most when texting your partner and explain why.
Tell your partner something you have never told anyone else.
Write your partner a one-sentence promise and read it aloud.
Name your partner's top three strengths without pausing.
Describe your partner using only adjectives for 30 seconds.
Whisper something kind that you have been meaning to say.

truth: What is the most embarrassing thing that has happened to us in public?
What is the weirdest food combination you have eaten that I do not know about?
Have you ever laughed at something I said and pretended not to?
What is a movie or show you have been secretly watching without me?
What is the most ridiculous thing we have argued about?
Have you ever re-gifted something I gave you?
What is the strangest dream you have had about me?
What is the most childish thing you still secretly enjoy?
What is a habit of mine that you find slightly annoying but have never said?
Have you ever pretended to be busy to avoid something I suggested?
What is the funniest inside joke we have?
What is something you used to do to impress me that you no longer bother with?
Have you ever fibbed about liking something just because I liked it?
What is the worst gift idea you almost gave me?
What is a nickname you call me in your head but never out loud?
What is the most ridiculous argument you have ever thought about starting?
What is the silliest thing that has ever made you cry in front of me?
Have you ever used me as an excuse to get out of something?
What is one thing you do when I am not around that you would not want me to see?
What is the most embarrassing song you know all the words to?
What is the weirdest compliment you have ever thought about giving me?
What is a fear you have that you find slightly ridiculous?
dare: Do your best impression of your partner for 30 seconds.
Let your partner style your hair however they want for the rest of the game.
Sing the chorus of the last song stuck in your head.
Text a mutual friend a ridiculous emoji sequence without explanation.
Do 10 jumping jacks while your partner counts out loud.
Speak in an accent for the next two rounds.
Let your partner read your most recent Google search aloud.
Tell a joke — if no one laughs, do five push-ups.
Let your partner change your phone wallpaper to a selfie of them.
Do your best runway walk across the room.
Describe your morning routine as if it were a nature documentary.
Let your partner send one text from your phone to anyone.
Do your best impression of how your partner eats.
Name five things in the room that are the same color as your partner's eyes.
Freestyle rap about your relationship for 20 seconds.
Let your partner put a temporary tattoo wherever they want.
Narrate the next five minutes of your life in third person.
Do an interpretive dance of how you felt on your first date.
Let your partner choose your phone ringtone for a week.
Say every word of a famous movie quote in the wrong order.
Let your partner write something on your arm with a marker.
Do your most embarrassing dance move for 15 seconds.

When do you feel most connected to me?

What is a kind gesture I could do for you that costs nothing?

dare: Look your partner in the eyes for 60 seconds without laughing.
Re-enact your favorite moment from your first date.
Slow dance together for one full song, even without music.
Write a one-paragraph story about your ideal future together.
Whisper something kind that you have been meaning to say.
Make a playlist of five songs that describe your relationship and name them.
Cook or order your partner's favorite meal tonight.
Tell your partner the top three things you are looking forward to doing with them.
Write a short poem about your partner — it only has to be four lines.
Create a short bucket list of three things you want to do together.
Hold eye contact and describe your partner using only positive words for 30 seconds.
Recreate the moment you first realized you liked your partner.
Plan a surprise date night and describe it in full detail right now.
Write your partner's name using your non-dominant hand and give it to them.
Say three things you are proud of your partner for.
Take a photo together that captures how you feel about each other.
Tell your partner one way they have made your life better.
Name one thing you are excited to do together that you have not done yet.
Describe your partner using only the titles of songs.
Hum your partner's favorite song while they guess it.
Tell your partner what you were thinking the first time you held their hand.
Give your partner a tour of your phone's photos and explain your favorite one of them.

dare: Plan a surprise date night and describe it in full detail right now.
Tell your partner the most attractive thing about them — do not hold back.
Write a one-paragraph story about your ideal future together and read it aloud.
Make a playlist of five songs that describe your feelings for your partner.
Give your partner a meaningful gift using only things already in the room.
Tell your partner one thing you have never told anyone else.
Describe to your partner in detail what you love about how they treat you.
Ask your partner one question you have always wanted to ask but never have.
Write your partner a letter they can open in a year.
Tell your partner five qualities that make them different from anyone you have dated.
Share a goal you have for your relationship that you have never said out loud.
Name three things about your partner that you want them to know you notice.

Tell your partner one thing you hope never changes between you.
Share one fear about the future that you think your partner could help you face.
